title: Classifying Policy Documents and Political Text
text: cat.pol thin domain wrapper around cat.stack adds: registered-source fetcher — 17 built-political data sources (municipal ordinances, federal laws, executive orders, presidential speeches, Truth Social posts, etc.) accessible via single source= argument. data lives HuggingFace refreshed weekly. Policy-document prompt framing — context like "policy document; identify affects" injected automatically. Everything else — supported models, output format, ensemble voting — identical cat.stack.

title: Classifying Policy Documents and Political Text
code: install.packages(   "cat.pol",   repos = c("https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev",             "https://cloud.r-project.org") ) library(cat.pol)

title: Classifying Policy Documents and Political Text
text: source maps curated HuggingFace dataset weekly updates. See Python catpol README current full list schema source.
code: list_sources() #> [1] "city_san_diego"            "city_san_francisco" #> [3] "city_los_angeles"          "federal_laws" #> [5] "federal_executive_orders"  "social_trump_truth" #> ...

title: Classifying Policy Documents and Political Text
text: returned data.frame one row per ordinance original text, date, URL/ID, one 0/1 column per category.
code: results <- classify(   source     = "city_san_diego",   doc_type   = "ordinance",   since      = "2024-01-01",   n          = 50L,   categories = c("Housing", "Public Safety", "Finance",                  "Infrastructure", "Health", "Other"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model = "gpt-4o-mini" )

what: Filter by date range and document type
title: Classifying Policy Documents and Political Text
code: # Resolutions only, between two dates: results <- classify(   source     = "city_san_francisco",   doc_type   = "resolution",   since      = "2024-06-01",   until      = "2024-12-31",   n          = 200L,   categories = c("Climate", "Housing", "Transportation",                  "Police accountability", "Other"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: Classifying Policy Documents and Political Text
text: policy documents registered sources (state legislation, agency rules, advocacy white papers), pass input_data: document_context cat.pol’s analog cat.survey’s survey_question — gives model framing documents analyzed.
code: results <- classify(   input_data       = df$bill_text,   document_context = "California state Senate bills, 2024 session",   categories       = c("Housing", "Public Safety", "Education",                        "Healthcare", "Environment", "Other"),   api_key          = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model       = "gpt-4o-mini" )

title: Classifying Policy Documents and Political Text
text: Long ordinances (10–20k words) can blow past context limits cost lot tokens. Summarize first, classify summaries: tone parameter specific cat.pol::summarize(); options include "eli5" (plain language), "neutral" (technical), "academic" (formal). Useful downstream readability generating press-friendly summaries alongside analytic classification.
code: summaries <- summarize(   source     = "city_san_diego",   doc_type   = "ordinance",   since      = "2024-01-01",   n          = 50L,   format     = "paragraph",   tone       = "eli5",                       # plain-language summary   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model = "gpt-4o-mini" )  results <- classify(   input_data = summaries$summary,   categories = c("Housing", "Public Safety", "Finance", "Other"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: Classifying Policy Documents and Political Text
text: specific category boundaries. Policy domains overlap — housing ordinance might also touch finance zoning. Either use multi-label (default) write categories explicit exclusion criteria. Watch ideological priors. LLMs political biases. research political lean classifier matters, use multi-model ensemble (see meta-package vignette, vignette("getting-started", "cat.llm")). Cite data source. source= value corresponds public HuggingFace dataset preferred citation.

title: Classifying Policy Documents and Political Text
text: Full Getting Started guide: vignette("getting-started", package = "cat.llm") Per-function reference: ?cat.pol::classify, ?cat.pol::extract, ?cat.pol::explore, ?cat.pol::summarize, ?cat.pol::list_sources Built-source schemas: https://github.com/chrissoria/cat-pol#built--sources

title: Authors and Citation
text: Chris Soria. Author, maintainer.

CatLLM: Reproducible Python Ecosystem Generating, Assigning, Scoring Open-Ended Text, Images, Documents Across Research Domains (v3.0.0) [Software]. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19960067

what: cat.pol — Policy and political-text classification for R
title: cat.pol — Policy and political-text classification for R
text: Political policy document classification LLMs. domain wrapper around cat.stack adds registered-source fetcher (city ordinances, federal laws, executive orders, presidential speeches, social-media archives) policy-document prompt framing. cat.pol wraps Python catpol package via reticulate.

title: cat.pol — Policy and political-text classification for R
code: # From R-universe (recommended once published): install.packages("cat.pol", repos = "https://chrissoria.r-universe.dev")  # Or from a local clone: devtools::install("path/to/cat.stack") devtools::install("path/to/cat.pol")  # Install the Python backend # pip install cat-pol

what: Classify ordinances from a built-in source
title: cat.pol — Policy and political-text classification for R
code: library(cat.pol)  results <- classify(   source     = "city_san_diego",   doc_type   = "ordinance",   since      = "2024-01-01",   n          = 50,   categories = c("Housing", "Public Safety", "Finance",                  "Infrastructure", "Health"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

what: Discover categories from your own text
title: cat.pol — Policy and political-text classification for R
code: result <- extract(   input_data        = df$bill_text,   document_context  = "California state legislation",   api_key           = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") ) print(result$top_categories)

what: Summarize policy documents in plain English
title: cat.pol — Policy and political-text classification for R
code: results <- summarize(   source = "federal_executive_orders",   since  = "2025-01-01",   format = "paragraph",   tone   = "eli5",   api_key = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") )

title: cat.pol — Policy and political-text classification for R
code: list_sources() #> [1] "city_san_diego"            "city_san_francisco" #> [3] "federal_laws"              "federal_executive_orders" #> [5] "social_trump_truth"        ...

title: cat.pol — Policy and political-text classification for R
text: MIT

what: Classify political and policy documents using LLMs — classify
title: Classify political and policy documents using LLMs — classify
text: Wraps Python catpol.classify() function. Can classify either raw text (via input_data) pull directly registered political data source (via source). catstack classification arguments supported.

title: Classify political and policy documents using LLMs — classify
code: classify(   categories,   input_data = NULL,   source = NULL,   doc_type = NULL,   since = NULL,   until = NULL,   n = NULL,   document_context = "",   description = "",   api_key = NULL,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   mode = "image",   creativity = NULL,   safety = FALSE,   chain_of_verification = FALSE,   chain_of_thought = FALSE,   step_back_prompt = FALSE,   context_prompt = FALSE,   thinking_budget = 0L,   example1 = NULL,   example2 = NULL,   example3 = NULL,   example4 = NULL,   example5 = NULL,   example6 = NULL,   filename = NULL,   save_directory = NULL,   model_source = "auto",   max_categories = 12L,   categories_per_chunk = 10L,   divisions = 10L,   research_question = NULL,   models = NULL,   consensus_threshold = "unanimous",   use_json_schema = TRUE,   max_workers = NULL,   fail_strategy = "partial",   max_retries = 5L,   batch_retries = 2L,   retry_delay = 1,   row_delay = 0,   pdf_dpi = 150L,   auto_download = FALSE,   add_other = "prompt",   check_verbosity = TRUE )

title: Classify political and policy documents using LLMs — classify
text: categories character vector category names. input_data character vector, list, data.frame column, NULL fetch registered source. Default NULL. source Character NULL. Registered source name (e.g. "city_san_diego", "federal_laws", "federal_executive_orders", "social_trump_truth"). Use list_sources() options. doc_type Character NULL. Filter source document type (e.g. "ordinance", "resolution"). since Character NULL. Earliest source row date (YYYY-MM-DD). Character NULL. Latest source row date (YYYY-MM-DD). n Integer NULL. Max number source rows classify. document_context Character. Context policy document analyzed. Default "". description Character. Additional context description. Default "". api_key Character NULL. API key LLM provider. user_model Character. Model name. Default "gpt-4o". mode Character. Processing mode. Default "image". creativity Numeric NULL. Temperature. Default NULL. safety Logical. Save progress item. Default FALSE. chain_of_verification Logical. Default FALSE. chain_of_thought Logical. Default FALSE. step_back_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. context_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. thinking_budget Integer. Default 0L. example1, example2, example3, example4, example5, example6 Optional -shot examples. filename Character NULL. Output CSV filename. save_directory Character NULL. Output directory. model_source Character. Provider hint. Default "auto". max_categories Integer. Default 12L. categories_per_chunk Integer. Default 10L. divisions Integer. Default 10L. research_question Character NULL. Optional research context. models List model specs ensemble mode. Default NULL. consensus_threshold Character numeric. Default "unanimous". use_json_schema Logical. Default TRUE. max_workers Integer NULL. Default NULL. fail_strategy Character. Default "partial". max_retries Integer. Default 5L. batch_retries Integer. Default 2L. retry_delay Numeric. Default 1.0. row_delay Numeric. Default 0.0. pdf_dpi Integer. Default 150L. auto_download Logical. Default FALSE. add_other Logical "prompt". Default "prompt". check_verbosity Logical. Default TRUE.

title: Classify political and policy documents using LLMs — classify
text: data.frame classification results.

title: Classify political and policy documents using LLMs — classify
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Pull recent San Diego ordinances from a registered source results <- classify(   source     = "city_san_diego",   doc_type   = "ordinance",   since      = "2024-01-01",   n          = 50L,   categories = c("Housing", "Public Safety", "Finance",                  "Infrastructure", "Health"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),   user_model = "gpt-4o-mini" )  # Or classify your own text directly results <- classify(   input_data = df$bill_text,   categories = c("Housing", "Public Safety", "Finance"),   api_key    = Sys.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") ) } # }

what: Explore raw categories in political and policy documents — explore
title: Explore raw categories in political and policy documents — explore

Default "". max_categories Integer. Default 12L. categories_per_chunk Integer. Default 10L. divisions Integer. Default 12L. user_model Character. Default "gpt-4o". creativity Numeric NULL. Default NULL. specificity Character. Default "broad". research_question Character NULL. filename Character NULL. model_source Character. Default "auto". iterations Integer. Default 8L. random_state Integer NULL. focus Character NULL. chunk_delay Numeric. Default 0.0.

what: Summarize political and policy documents using LLMs — summarize
title: Summarize political and policy documents using LLMs — summarize
text: Wraps Python catpol.summarize() function. Generates summaries policy text registered political data source. Adds tone parameter policy-specific framing.

title: Summarize political and policy documents using LLMs — summarize
code: summarize(   input_data = NULL,   source = NULL,   doc_type = NULL,   since = NULL,   until = NULL,   n = NULL,   format = "paragraph",   tone = "eli5",   api_key = NULL,   description = "",   instructions = "",   max_length = NULL,   focus = NULL,   user_model = "gpt-4o",   model_source = "auto",   mode = "image",   input_mode = NULL,   input_type = "auto",   pdf_dpi = 150L,   creativity = NULL,   thinking_budget = 0L,   chain_of_thought = TRUE,   context_prompt = FALSE,   step_back_prompt = FALSE,   filename = NULL,   save_directory = NULL,   models = NULL,   max_workers = NULL,   parallel = NULL,   auto_download = FALSE,   safety = FALSE,   max_retries = 5L,   batch_retries = 2L,   retry_delay = 1,   row_delay = 0,   fail_strategy = "partial",   batch_mode = FALSE,   batch_poll_interval = 30,   batch_timeout = 86400 )

title: Summarize political and policy documents using LLMs — summarize
text: input_data character vector, list, PDF/URL paths; NULL fetch registered source. source Character NULL. Registered source name. doc_type Character NULL. Filter source document type. since Character NULL. Earliest source row date (YYYY-MM-DD). Character NULL. Latest source row date (YYYY-MM-DD). n Integer NULL. Max number source rows. format Character. Output format. Default "paragraph". tone Character. Policy-specific tone, e.g. "eli5", "neutral", "academic". Default "eli5". api_key Character NULL. API key LLM provider. description Character. Default "". instructions Character. Specific instructions summary. Default "". max_length Integer NULL. Default NULL. focus Character NULL. Default NULL. user_model Character. Default "gpt-4o". model_source Character. Default "auto". mode Character. Default "image". input_mode Character NULL. Default NULL. input_type Character. Default "auto". pdf_dpi Integer. Default 150L. creativity Numeric NULL. Default NULL. thinking_budget Integer. Default 0L. chain_of_thought Logical. Default TRUE. context_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. step_back_prompt Logical. Default FALSE. filename Character NULL. save_directory Character NULL. models List model specs ensemble mode. Default NULL. max_workers Integer NULL. Default NULL. parallel Logical NULL. Default NULL. auto_download Logical. Default FALSE. safety Logical. Default FALSE. max_retries Integer. Default 5L. batch_retries Integer. Default 2L. retry_delay Numeric. Default 1.0. row_delay Numeric. Default 0.0. fail_strategy Character. Default "partial". batch_mode Logical. Default FALSE. batch_poll_interval Numeric. Default 30.0. batch_timeout Numeric. Default 86400.0.

title: cat.pol 0.1.0
text: Initial release R interface Python catpol package. Domain wrapper around cat.stack adds registered-source fetcher (city ordinances, federal laws, executive orders, presidential speeches, social-media archives) policy-document prompt framing. Exports classify(), extract(), explore(), summarize(), list_sources().
